token_self_service_browser.rationale: Possession of a current one-time token, not campaign role, controls the setup/reset flow.

view_as_admin_api.rationale: View As is an app-admin capability evaluated against the real authenticated actor.

admin_browser.rationale: Admin surfaces do not become player-visible through campaign visibility settings.

admin_api.rationale: Admin API access is independent of campaign visibility.

campaign_admin_browser.rationale: The campaign-scoped control requires a real app admin and preserves not-found handling for a missing campaign.

campaign_catalog_browser.rationale: The response filters campaigns through each campaign's effective campaign-scope visibility.

campaign_catalog_api.rationale: The response filters campaigns through each campaign's effective campaign-scope visibility.

campaign_read_browser.rationale: Access follows the effective campaign visibility, including the campaign privacy floor.

campaign_read_api.rationale: Access follows the effective campaign visibility, including the campaign privacy floor.

wiki_read_browser.rationale: Access follows the effective wiki visibility and campaign privacy floor; the requested published object must also be visible.

wiki_asset_read_browser.rationale: Access follows the effective wiki visibility and campaign privacy floor; any existing contained campaign asset is readable, including assets not linked from a visible published page.

wiki_read_api.rationale: Access follows the effective wiki visibility and campaign privacy floor; the requested published object must also be visible.

systems_read_browser.rationale: Access follows the effective Systems visibility and campaign privacy floor.

systems_read_api.rationale: Access follows the effective Systems visibility and campaign privacy floor.

systems_source_read_browser.rationale: The enabled source visibility is clamped by the effective Systems scope and campaign privacy floor.

systems_source_read_api.rationale: The enabled source visibility is clamped by the effective Systems scope and campaign privacy floor.

systems_entry_read_browser.rationale: The source must be enabled. Within that source, enabled entry visibility, the Systems scope, and campaign privacy floor apply unless the effective actor is a direct app admin. View As replaces the effective actor and never retains this bypass.

systems_entry_read_api.rationale: Enabled entry visibility, source clamp, Systems scope, and the campaign privacy floor apply unless the effective actor is a direct app admin, who may inspect a stored entry even through a disabled source. View As replaces the effective actor and never retains this bypass.

session_read_browser.rationale: Access follows the effective Session visibility and campaign privacy floor; unrevealed or management-only objects remain hidden.

session_live_state_browser.rationale: The default and Character polling modes follow Session-scope visibility; the same route with view=dm additionally requires campaign DM/admin Session-management authority and returns 403 otherwise.

session_read_api.rationale: Access follows the effective Session visibility and campaign privacy floor; unrevealed or management-only objects remain hidden.

session_character_read_browser.rationale: Session Character first requires Session-scope access, then limits selectable records to the assigned player's characters or all records for a campaign DM/admin.

combat_read_browser.rationale: Access follows the effective Combat visibility; role and combatant detail flags filter the projection.

combat_read_api.rationale: Access follows the effective Combat visibility; role and combatant detail flags filter the projection.

campaign_manage_browser.rationale: Campaign management requires DM/admin authority and does not become player-visible through scope visibility.

campaign_manage_api.rationale: Campaign management requires DM/admin authority and does not become player-visible through scope visibility.

combat_systems_manager_browser.rationale: Systems-monster Combat tools require DM/admin Combat management authority and access to the campaign Systems scope.

combat_systems_manager_api.rationale: Systems-monster Combat tools require DM/admin Combat management authority and access to the campaign Systems scope.

systems_manage_browser.rationale: Systems management requires effective Systems-scope access plus campaign DM/admin authority.

systems_manage_api.rationale: Systems management requires effective Systems-scope access plus campaign DM/admin authority.

dm_content_systems_manage_api.rationale: The endpoint first requires effective DM Content-scope admission, then requires Systems-manager authority; a non-admin campaign DM must also have effective Systems-scope access. The manifest has one scalar campaign_scope field, so the inner Systems scope is recorded here rather than replacing the outer dm_content scope.

systems_shared_editor_browser.rationale: Shared/core editing is app-admin by default, and the early app-admin branch intentionally bypasses campaign scope and opt-in checks. A campaign DM requires effective Systems-scope access plus the explicit shared-core permission policy.

character_read_browser.rationale: Full browser roster, detail, and portrait reads require effective Characters-scope access; assignment does not bypass that browser scope gate.

character_read_api.rationale: Character visibility follows the scope, while an assigned player can receive the bounded assigned-character projection.

character_owner_or_manager_browser.rationale: Mutable character state is limited to the assigned player, campaign DM, or app admin.

character_owner_or_manager_api.rationale: Mutable character state is limited to the assigned player, campaign DM, or app admin.

character_scope_owner_or_manager_browser.rationale: The route first requires Characters-scope access, then limits the target to the assigned player or a campaign DM/admin.

character_scope_owner_or_manager_api.rationale: The route first requires Characters-scope access, then limits the target to the assigned player or a campaign DM/admin.

cultivation_manager_browser.rationale: Cultivation requires Characters-scope access plus campaign Session-manager authority, which is limited to an eligible campaign DM/admin.

cultivation_manager_api.rationale: Cultivation requires Characters-scope access plus campaign Session-manager authority, which is limited to an eligible campaign DM/admin.

character_manager_browser.rationale: Stable-definition authoring and destructive character management require campaign DM/admin authority.

character_manager_api.rationale: Stable-definition authoring and destructive character management require campaign DM/admin authority.

character_admin_browser.rationale: Character owner assignment controls are app-admin-only and independent of player visibility.

character_admin_api.rationale: Character owner assignment controls are app-admin-only and independent of player visibility.

session_participant_browser.rationale: Posting requires Session-scope access plus an active player/DM/admin role; observers cannot post.

session_participant_api.rationale: Posting requires Session-scope access plus an active player/DM/admin role; observers cannot post.

combat_character_read_browser.rationale: The selected-PC combat projection is available to its assigned player and campaign managers within Combat scope.

combat_character_edit_browser.rationale: Combat character mutations require the selected tracked-PC relationship plus assignment or campaign-manager authority.

dm_content_read_browser.rationale: Any actor admitted by effective DM Content scope can read stored statblock bodies and custom-condition names, descriptions, and counts. Templates hide mutation controls from non-managers but do not filter those records; the Systems subpage still requires Systems-manager authority.

dm_content_read_api.rationale: The DM Content summary follows scope visibility while management-only counts and records remain permission-filtered.

api.combat_add_systems_monster.rationale: The DM/admin action reads one accessible Systems monster and then durably adds a Combat combatant.

api.combat_search_systems_monsters.rationale: This is a read-only DM/admin Combat lookup against the accessible campaign Systems monster catalog.

campaign_combat_add_systems_monster.rationale: The DM/admin action reads one accessible Systems monster and then durably adds a Combat combatant.

campaign_combat_search_systems_monsters.rationale: This is a read-only DM/admin Combat lookup against the accessible campaign Systems monster catalog.

campaign_dm_content_add_condition_defini.rationale: The route first requires effective DM Content-scope access, then requires DM/admin management authority.

campaign_dm_content_delete_condition_def.rationale: The route first requires effective DM Content-scope access, then requires DM/admin management authority.

campaign_dm_content_delete_statblock.rationale: The route first requires effective DM Content-scope access, then requires DM/admin management authority.

campaign_dm_content_update_condition_def.rationale: The route first requires effective DM Content-scope access, then requires DM/admin management authority.

campaign_dm_content_update_statblock.rationale: The route first requires effective DM Content-scope access, then requires DM/admin management authority.

campaign_dm_content_upload_statblock.rationale: The route first requires effective DM Content-scope access, then requires DM/admin management authority; statblock upload is available only for DND-5E campaigns.
